title: Podman socket dies (Failed with result 'trigger-limit-hit + XDG_RUNTIME_DIR issues')
body:
### Issue Description

Rootless podman socket dies due to trigger-limit-hit and cannot be used after restart (it dies again, possibly related to parsing of XDG_RUNTIME_DIR?). Podman still runs fine for all other users including root.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

1. Install podman, podman-compose, podman-docker, crun, netaverk
2. Configure crun and netaverk
3. Configure rootless podman including sockets
4. Run podman-compose for a couple of weeks, lots of iterations, rebuilding, restarting
5. Socket started to die (trigger-limit-hit) when mounting the socket in a podman container
6. Cannot make socket to work by restarting, it dies again when trying to connect


### Describe the results you received

systemctl --user restart podman.socket
systemctl --user status podman.socket

```
● podman.socket - Podman API Socket
   Loaded: loaded (/usr/lib/systemd/user/podman.socket; enabled; vendor preset: enabled)
   Active: active (listening) since Sat 2024-02-24 13:25:12 CET; 1s ago
     Docs: man:podman-system-service(1)
   Listen: /run/user/12437/podman/podman.sock (Stream)
   CGroup: /user.slice/user-12437.slice/user@12437.service/podman.socket

Feb 24 13:25:12 xsedlabd501.se.shb.biz systemd[45869]: Listening on Podman API Socket.
```
 curl --unix-socket $XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/podman/podman.sock -H 'content-type: application/json' http://localhost/_ping
`curl: (56) Recv failure: Connection reset by peer`

systemctl --user status podman.socket

```
● podman.socket - Podman API Socket
   Loaded: loaded (/usr/lib/systemd/user/podman.socket; enabled; vendor preset: enabled)
   Active: failed (Result: trigger-limit-hit) since Sat 2024-02-24 13:26:56 CET; 48s ago
     Docs: man:podman-system-service(1)
   Listen: /run/user/12437/podman/podman.sock (Stream)

Feb 24 13:25:12 xsedlabd501.se.shb.biz systemd[45869]: Listening on Podman API Socket.
Feb 24 13:26:56 xsedlabd501.se.shb.biz systemd[45869]: podman.socket: Trigger limit hit, refusing further activation.
Feb 24 13:26:56 xsedlabd501.se.shb.biz systemd[45869]: podman.socket: Failed with result 'trigger-limit-hit'.
```

### Describe the results you expected

podman.socket should not die

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

RHEL8 on VMWare, SELinux=Permissive

### Additional information

I can see from the logs that this might be related to XDG_RUNTIME_DIR

```
Feb 26 07:51:35 HIDDEN systemd[2130]: Starting Podman API Service...
Feb 26 07:51:35 HIDDEN systemd[2130]: Started Podman API Service.
Feb 26 07:51:35 HIDDEN podman[5361]: time="2024-02-26T07:51:35+01:00" level=error msg="XDG_RUNTIME_DIR directory \"/run/user\" is not owned by the current user"
Feb 26 07:51:35 HIDDEN systemd[2130]: podman.service: Main process exited, code=exited, status=1/FAILURE
Feb 26 07:51:35 HIDDEN systemd[2130]: podman.service: Failed with result 'exit-code'.
Feb 26 07:51:35 HIDDEN systemd[2130]: Starting Podman API Service...
Feb 26 07:51:35 HIDDEN systemd[2130]: Started Podman API Service.
Feb 26 07:51:35 HIDDEN podman[5368]: time="2024-02-26T07:51:35+01:00" level=error msg="XDG_RUNTIME_DIR directory \"/run/user\" is not owned by the current user"
Feb 26 07:51:35 HIDDEN systemd[2130]: podman.service: Main process exited, code=exited, status=1/FAILURE
Feb 26 07:51:35 HIDDEN systemd[2130]: podman.service: Failed with result 'exit-code'.
Feb 26 07:51:35 HIDDEN systemd[2130]: Starting Podman API Service...
Feb 26 07:51:35 HIDDEN systemd[2130]: Started Podman API Service.
Feb 26 07:51:35 HIDDEN podman[5376]: time="2024-02-26T07:51:35+01:00" level=error msg="XDG_RUNTIME_DIR directory \"/run/user\" is not owned by the current user"
Feb 26 07:51:35 HIDDEN systemd[2130]: podman.service: Main process exited, code=exited, status=1/FAILURE
Feb 26 07:51:35 HIDDEN systemd[2130]: podman.service: Failed with result 'exit-code'.
Feb 26 07:51:35 HIDDEN systemd[2130]: Starting Podman API Service...
Feb 26 07:51:35 HIDDEN systemd[2130]: Started Podman API Service.
Feb 26 07:51:35 HIDDEN podman[5383]: time="2024-02-26T07:51:35+01:00" level=error msg="XDG_RUNTIME_DIR directory \"/run/user\" is not owned by the current user"
Feb 26 07:51:35 HIDDEN systemd[2130]: podman.service: Main process exited, code=exited, status=1/FAILURE
Feb 26 07:51:35 HIDDEN systemd[2130]: podman.service: Failed with result 'exit-code'.
Feb 26 07:51:35 HIDDEN systemd[2130]: podman.socket: Trigger limit hit, refusing further activation.
Feb 26 07:51:35 HIDDEN systemd[2130]: podman.socket: Failed with result 'trigger-limit-hit'.
```

From ~/.bashrc

```
# User specific aliases and functions
export XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/run/user/$(id -u)
```

Shouldn't the message include the user id, like /run/user/####?

msg="XDG_RUNTIME_DIR **directory \"/run/user\"** is not owned by the current user"

comments.body: This is now solved. The issue was that XDG_RUNTIME_DIR was set in /etc/environment to XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/run/user/$UID. Removing that line + reboot solved the problem.
